description:
## Background

Issue Detail currently shows a selected Issue's metadata, description, dependencies, and comments but not its hierarchy. Beadwork stores this relationship on each Child Issue through its `parent` field. Beadsmith already loads all Workspace Issues from `bw list --all --json`; deriving children from that structured data preserves Beadwork as the source of truth and requires no parsing of `bw show`'s human-readable output.

Triage: ready-for-agent

## What to build

Make the first vertical slice of Child Issue visibility demoable: every selected Issue's Issue Detail renders a Child Issues section directly after Dependencies. The section lists all loaded Child Issues whose `parent` matches the selected Issue ID, in the loaded order. Each entry displays ID, status, and title in that order. When there are no Child Issues, retain the section and render an explicit empty state rather than omitting it.

## Technical Approach

- Derive Child Issues only from the successful explorer state's `allIssues` collection by matching the Beadwork `parent` field.
- Keep the existing selected-Issue behavior and active Issue List View behavior unchanged in this slice.
- Reuse the existing Issue view-model status labels and badge styling where appropriate.
- Render the new section after the Dependencies section in `IssueDetail` with accessible semantic list markup.

## Considerations

- Do not add a `children` field to the Beadwork/RPC Issue contract and do not issue an additional `bw` command.
- Do not parse the human-readable `bw show` output; ADR-0003 requires structured CLI output.
- This slice deliberately preserves source order and the visible empty state. Ordering and hiding are separate follow-up work.
- A Child Issue is a loaded Issue whose `parent` references the selected Issue; use that project glossary terminology.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Issue Detail shows a Child Issues section immediately after Dependencies for every selected Issue.
- [ ] The section derives entries from `allIssues` whose `parent` equals the selected Issue ID.
- [ ] Each Child Issue entry displays its ID, status, then title, in loaded order.
- [ ] An Issue with no Child Issues shows an explicit empty state in the section.
- [ ] Focused unit/component tests cover matching children, no children, display content/order, and placement.

## Test Plan

- Add pure derivation tests for matching by `parent` and preserving loaded order.
- Extend Issue Explorer component tests for populated and empty Child Issues sections.
- Run the affected frontend test suite and Ultracite checks.
title: Derive and display Child Issues in Issue Detail
